name: healthcare_ransomware_incident_forces_lumen_tru_loy
premise: A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: healthcare ransomware incident forces lumen tru loy. The moral tension is explicitly framed as truth versus loyalty: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. A union steward and a patient-family advocate are both on the bridge demanding incompatible assurances. An automated failover or batch commit fires in twenty-two minutes unless a named approver intervenes. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Marco Navarro
agents.identity: I am Marco Navarro, the accountable decision-maker on this Healthcare bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes loyalty whenever someone invokes truth.

agents.name: Mira Berg
agents.identity: I am Mira Berg, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so loyalty is not sacrificed by default just because truth feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect loyalty.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Marco Navarro must decide under truth versus loyalty. What does Marco Navarro do?
